description: Core detection engine, basic dashboard, risk scoring, and blocking rules (no CAPTCHA).

userStories.title: As a security operator, I want to define detection rules so that the system can evaluate incoming requests.
acceptanceCriteria: A JSON/YAML rule file exists in the configured location.
Each rule includes a unique ID, condition type (IP reputation, User‑Agent, header, rate‑limit), threshold, and action (allow, rate‑limit, block).
The rule file can be reloaded without restarting the detection engine.

userStories.title: As a security operator, I want the detection engine to evaluate every request against the rules and produce a risk score.
acceptanceCriteria: The engine receives request metadata (IP, User‑Agent, headers, timestamps) from the proxy.
For each request, the engine computes a numeric risk score (0‑100) based on rule matches.
The engine returns a verdict: allow, rate‑limit, or block, based on configurable score thresholds.
The verdict and score are logged with request metadata.

userStories.title: As a security operator, I want a lightweight reverse‑proxy that forwards traffic to the detection engine and enforces the engine's verdict.
acceptanceCriteria: All incoming HTTP(S) requests pass through the proxy before reaching the web application.
The proxy forwards request metadata to the detection engine via HTTP/REST or gRPC.
Based on the verdict, the proxy either forwards the request, applies rate limiting, or returns HTTP 403/429.
All decisions are logged for audit purposes.

userStories.title: As a security operator, I want a minimal dashboard that shows real‑time traffic overview and blocked request statistics.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard displays total requests per second, % of allowed vs. blocked traffic, and top 5 offending IPs.
Data updates at least once per second via WebSocket or polling.
Dashboard is accessible via HTTPS with token‑based authentication.
Dashboard UI is built with React and uses a simple charting library.

userStories.title: As a security operator, I need an API to retrieve and manage detection rules, allow‑list, and deny‑list entries.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /rules returns the current rule set.
POST /rules adds a new rule; PUT /rules/:id updates an existing rule; DELETE /rules/:id removes a rule.
GET /allowlist, POST /allowlist, DELETE /allowlist/:ip perform similar CRUD for trusted IPs.
GET /denylist, POST /denylist, DELETE /denylist/:ip perform similar CRUD for blocked IPs.
All API endpoints require a bearer token.

userStories.title: As a QA engineer, I want automated tests that verify core detection, proxy behavior, and dashboard data.
acceptanceCriteria: Unit tests for each rule type (IP reputation, UA, header, rate‑limit).
Integration test that sends synthetic requests through the proxy and asserts correct verdicts.
End‑to‑end test that verifies the dashboard reflects the expected metrics after simulated traffic.
All tests run in CI and must pass before merge.

tasks.description: Create core detection rule schema and sample rule file.
Design detection engine architecture and define API contract with proxy.
Implement traffic interception proxy (NGINX‑Lua or Envoy) that forwards metadata to detection engine.
Develop core detection engine: load rules, compute risk score, return verdict.
Create PostgreSQL schema for logs, rules, allow/deny lists, and write persistence layer.
Build minimal dashboard UI (React) showing real‑time RPS, allowed %/blocked %, top offending IPs.
Implement backend API endpoints for metrics, rule CRUD, and allow/deny list management.
Write automated test suite (unit + integration + e2e) covering core detection, proxy behavior, and dashboard updates.
Run iteration‑1 QA, capture results, and fix any defects.

notes: All work assumes existing CI/CD pipeline and containerised deployment environment. No CAPTCHA implementation is included in this iteration.
